73.reason: [The loop `while(~scanf("%d",&type))` will read until EOF, but the problem specifies only one vertex to delete. This causes `del` to be incremented multiple times and overwrites `graph_del` incorrectly for multiple inputs, leading to logical errors. Also, `del` is used as a count, not the actual deleted vertex, which breaks traversal logic.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop `while(~scanf("%d",&type))` will read until EOF, but the problem specifies only one vertex to delete. This causes `del` to be incremented multiple times and overwrites `graph_del` incorrectly for multiple inputs, leading to logical errors. Also, `del` is used as a count, not the actual deleted vertex, which breaks traversal logic.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop condition `while(~scanf("%d",&type))` will keep reading until EOF, but the problem specifies only one vertex to delete. This causes `del` to increment multiple times and corrupts the graph deletion logic. Also, `memset` is used without including <string.h>, causing a compilation error.]
